name: Plana de Vic Loop: Vic, Folgueroles, and Tona
name_original: Castillo de Tona y Santa Maria de Folgueroles - circular por la plana de Vic
description: This moderate 25.3-mile touring bicycle loop explores the Plana de Vic, connecting Vic, Folgueroles, and Tona with 1311 feet of gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vic features a landscape characterized by the Plana de Vic, a central plain surrounded by hills and mountains. Routes often traverse agricultural areas, river valleys, and ascend into higher elevations, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region includes natural features such as the Sau Reservoir and numerous historic churches and hermitages. Best touring cycling routes around Vic The most popular touring cycling route is Plana de Vic Loop: Vic, Folgueroles, and Tona, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route explores the flat and gently rolling terrain of the Vic plain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is El Pont d'en Bruguer – Sant Joan de Fàbregues Church loop from Vic, a difficult 48.3 miles (77.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading through hilly landscapes to the Sant Joan de Fàbregues Church. Local touring cyclists also love the Church of Sant Sadurní d'Osormort – Revell Pass loop from Vic, a 40.5 miles (65.3 km) trail leading through the Revell Pass, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Vic is defined by plains, hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops across the plain to challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vic's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Vic?
A: Vic offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 140 different tours to explore. This includes a variety of options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Vic?
A: The terrain around Vic is quite diverse. You'll find routes that traverse the flat and gently rolling Plana de Vic, agricultural areas, and river valleys. Other routes ascend into higher elevations, offering more challenging climbs through hilly landscapes and mountainous areas.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Vic?
A: Yes, Vic has several touring cycling routes suitable for beginners. While many routes involve some elevation, there are 11 routes classified as easy. These typically stick to the flatter sections of the Plana de Vic, offering a gentler introduction to touring cycling in the region.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Vic?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Vic offers 83 difficult routes. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pont d'en Bruguer – Sant Joan de Fàbregues Church loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain over 77.7 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40344186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Sadurní d'Osormort – Revell Pass loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;, covering 65.3 km with substantial climbs through the Revell Pass.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Vic?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Vic are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971633840/plana-de-vic-loop-vic-folgueroles-and-tona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana de Vic Loop: Vic, Folgueroles, and Tona&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic circular tour of the plain.

Q: What are the main attractions or points of interest I can see along the touring cycling routes in Vic?
A: Along the touring cycling routes in Vic, you can explore a variety of attractions. Natural features include the scenic Sau Reservoir, which is part of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10476436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sau Reservoir – Main Square of Vic loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt;. You can also discover numerous historic churches and hermitages. Additionally, the region features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3792648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Boix Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5079539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Tona&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1116105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Omeda Air Raid Shelters&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Vic?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vic are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the peaceful plains to the challenging climbs, and the opportunity to discover historic sites and natural beauty like the Sau Reservoir.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in Vic?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vic vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971633840/plana-de-vic-loop-vic-folgueroles-and-tona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana de Vic Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 40.8 km (approx. 2 hours 50 minutes), to much longer and more challenging rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5335300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pont d'en Bruguer – Sant Joan de Fàbregues Church loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 77.7 km and can take over 5 hours.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Sau Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Sau Reservoir is a prominent natural feature in the Vic region, and there are touring cycling routes that incorporate it. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10476436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sau Reservoir – Main Square of Vic loop from Vic&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 47.1 km route that offers scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: Are there historical sites or churches to explore on the routes?
A: Absolutely. The Vic region is rich in history, and many touring cycling routes pass by or lead to historical sites. You can visit numerous historic churches and hermitages, such as the Sant Joan de Fàbregues Church or the Church of Sant Sadurní d'Osormort, which are featured in several challenging loops.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Vic?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Vic generally depends on your preference for weather and terrain. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring both the plains and the hillier sections. Summers can be warm, especially in the open plains, while winters can be cooler, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Is it possible to access touring cycling routes in Vic using public transport?
A: Vic is well-connected by public transport, particularly by train and bus services, which can facilitate access to the region. Many routes start and end in Vic itself, making it convenient to reach by public transport. However, specific public transport options to individual trailheads may vary, so it's advisable to check local schedules.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Vic?
A: For routes starting directly from Vic, you'll find various parking options within the town. For routes that begin in smaller villages or more remote areas, parking is often available near the starting points, such as public car parks or designated spaces. It's always a good idea to check the specific route details for recommended parking locations.

Q: Are there options for food and drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Vic pass through or near charming towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The region is known for its local cuisine, offering opportunities to enjoy traditional meals and snacks. Planning your route with komoot can help you identify these stops in advance.

Q: Are the touring cycling routes in Vic suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Vic involve significant elevation, there are 11 easy routes that are generally more suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically stick to the flatter sections of the Plana de Vic, offering a pleasant experience for all ages. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.
tips:
It's worth a visit.

It has a square shape and is the largest in the city. On all four sides, there is a series of arcades that make the entire turn of the square. In the middle is a large, open, unpaved space where fairs, markets, and concerts are held. On the southeast side we find the town hall, a Gothic building with a bell tower dating from the year 1388.
Beautiful Romanesque bridge from the 14th century. Busy for both walkers and cyclists. Passage point of the royal road from Vic to Olot and the Camí de St. James

Religious sanctuary located in the main square of Folgueroles (Osona) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.
The current temple has a single nave with a semicircular apse. Originally it appears to have had three apses, two of which were demolished in the 17th century
Located in the Pla del Castell, on a small hill at the foot of the center of Tona, you will find La Torre Mayor. The tower is the only part of the Tona Castle that remains standing.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle loop, covering approximately 40.8 km, usually takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Plana de Vic Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this loop is varied. While much of the Plana de Vic area is generally quite flat, this specific route includes some unpaved and challenging sections. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, requiring good fitness for the moderate difficulty rating.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route requires good fitness due to its length and some challenging unpaved sections. While the broader Plana de Vic area has options for beginners, this specific loop is best suited for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Plana de Vic Loop?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through charming towns and enjoy picturesque landscapes. Notable highlights include the Main Square of Vic, El Pont d'en Bruguer, the Church of Santa María de Folgueroles, and the Church of Sant Andreu del Castell. The route also offers views of the Montseny massif and the distant Catalan Pyrenees.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: Vic serves as a central hub for routes in the area. The Alberg Canonge Collell in Vic is a great starting point, offering amenities like bike rentals, washing facilities, secure parking, and repair shops. You can find parking options within Vic, especially near the town center or the Alberg.

faqs.question: Is the Plana de Vic Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through rural areas and towns, specific regulations for dogs on this touring bicycle route are not explicitly stated. If cycling with a dog, it's generally best to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock, and ensure they are comfortable with the distance and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Plana de Vic area is particularly vibrant in spring and autumn, offering a delightful array of colors and pleasant cycling conditions. These seasons are generally ideal to experience the picturesque landscapes.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It is part of an extensive network of signposted routes open to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Vic, Folgueroles, and Tona, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and water stops. Vic, in particular, offers many amenities. The Alberg Canonge Collell in Vic also provides various services for cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Vic – Mont-rodon – Puig l’Agulla – Sant Julià de Vilatorta – Calldetenes – Vic, Vic – Malla - Tona – Múnter – La Guixa – Vic, and Via cicloturística InterCatalunya trails, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a touring bicycle route with varied terrain, including some unpaved and challenging sections, a touring bicycle or a hybrid bike with good tires would be ideal. A mountain bike would also be suitable for comfort on the unpaved parts.
